description: Parsed techniques from how2heap C sources

techniques.description:
demonstrates how to recover the original content from a poisoned
	 * value because of the safe-linking mechanism.
This file demonstrates a simple double-free attack with fastbins.

the following five places:
1.
VULNERABILITY

The following operation assumes the address of victim is known, thus requiring
	// a heap leak.

heap_operations.line: printf("First we need to free(allocsize) at least 7 times to fill the tcache.\n"

techniques.description:
This file demonstrates a powerful tcache poisoning attack by tricking malloc into");
    puts("returning a pointer to an arbitrary location (in this demo, the stack).

The following restriction for normal bins won't allow us to create chunks bigger than the memory
	 * allocated from the system in this arena:
	 *
	 * https://sourceware.

This is a revisited version that bypass also the hardening check introduced by glibc malloc\n");
  fprintf(stderr, "This is tested against Ubuntu 20.

heap_operations.line: for(int i=0; i<7; i++) dummies[i] = malloc(0x100);
victim[1] = (intptr_t)stack_buffer_1; // victim->bk is pointing to stack

techniques.description:
the following things: 
- Finds a valid arena location for a non-main arena.

heap_operations.line: fake_heap_info[0] = (uint64_t) fake_arena; // Setting the fake ar_ptr (arena)
chunk_ptr[1] = 0x60 | 0x4; // Setting the non-main arena bit

techniques.description: This file demonstrates the house of spirit attack.
house_of_tangerine technique
Since glibc2.30, two new checks have been enforced on large bin chunk insertion\n\n

This is another simple chunks overlapping problem\n");
	printf("The previous technique is killed by patch: https://sourceware.
VULNERABILITY

the following:
 *	
 *                                  (ptr^key)^key = ptr
 *
 * The technique requires control over the t-cache metadata, so pairing it with a technique such as
 * house of water might be favourable.

This is achieved through sysmalloc calling _int_free to the top_chunk (wilderness),
 * if the top_chunk can't be merged during heap growth
 * https://elixir.
This file demonstrates the house of spirit attack on tcache.

demonstrates what an attacker can achieve by controlling\n"
       "the metadata chunk of the tcache.

This file demonstrates a simple tcache poisoning attack by tricking malloc into\n"
		   "returning a pointer to an arbitrary location (in this case, the stack).
This file demonstrates TCache relative write, a technique used to achieve arbitrary decimal writing and chunk pointer arbitrary write on heap.

heap_operations.line: free(p1);	// In this PoC, we use p1 simply for a libc leak
